name: Sheila Jordan
abstract: Sheila Jeannette Jordan (née Dawson; November 18, 1928 – August 11, 2025) was an American jazz singer and songwriter. She recorded as a session musician with an array of critically acclaimed artists in addition to recording her own albums.

interviews.title: Sheila Jordan interviewed by Monk Rowe, Middleburgh, New York, May 11, 2002
Sheila Jordan — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

concerts.label: The Harvie Swartz String Ensemble with Erik Friedlander and Terry King

works.t: I Miss That Old Time Feeling
Let's Face the Music and Dance
Sophisticated Ladies: It Don't Mean a Thing
I Get Along Without You Very Well

about_quotes.speaker: Tom Varner
about_quotes.evidence: speaking of Sheila Jordan – we both played in the George Gruntz big band.
about_quotes.doc_title: Tom Varner interviewed by Monk Rowe, via Zoom, August 13, 2025

about_quotes.speaker: Bill Kirchner
about_quotes.evidence: one of them is doing the Chicago Jazz Festival in 1987 with my nonet and Sheila Jordan as a guest vocalist.
about_quotes.doc_title: Bill Kirchner interviewed by Monk Rowe, via Zoom, June 4, 2021

about_quotes.speaker: Barry Harris
about_quotes.evidence: I left out Sheila Jordan, you know. I left out Skeeter, you know these are people who are all important to me. And when I went to New York for the first time – me and Doug went together, we stayed with Sheila Jordan, you know.
about_quotes.doc_title: Barry Harris — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Ruthie Foster
about_quotes.evidence: [INTERVIEWER] The jazz singer Sheila Jordan talked about very rare occurrences where she left her body and she was as if she was watching herself sing. [INTERVIEWEE] It’s very much that.
about_quotes.doc_title: Ruthie Foster interviewed by Monk Rowe, via Zoom, February 28, 2023

about_quotes.speaker: Tim Ries
about_quotes.evidence: [INTERVIEWER] Sheila Jordan told me a pretty horrific story... did you witness anything like that going to those clubs with your father?
about_quotes.doc_title: Tim Ries interviewed by Monk Rowe, Utica, New York, March 19, 2022

about_quotes.speaker: Genevieve Rose
about_quotes.evidence: I’ve got something that should be, I think it’s October 17th with Sheila Jordan and Harold Danko as a piano player
about_quotes.doc_title: Genevieve Rose interviewed by Monk Rowe, Clinton, New York, March 14, 1998
